Try HARD to REFUTE this proposed change to the POLARIS pipeline. Default refuted=true if uncertain. Read code at /home/polaris/wt/flywheel if needed.

CHANGE: Light the dark synthesis lane (zero new code) (C1)
FILE/SEAM: /home/polaris/wt/flywheel/src/polaris_graph/generator/analyst_synthesis_deviation_check.py:155-203 (gates), /home/polaris/wt/flywheel/src/polaris_graph/generator/analyst_synthesis.py:672-695 (emitter fail-closed) :: The deliberate cross-source lane is built, empirically motivated (its own comments cite the 79/81 over-drop), and OFF three flags deep. The emitter at analyst_synthesis.py:685-695 fail-closes to ('',0,0) in stock config, so the layer runs every night and emits nothing.
WHAT: Env-only arm flip, no code: set PG_ANALYST_SYNTHESIS_PROMOTE_GROUNDED=1, PG_ANALYST_SYNTHESIS_BASKET_MODE=1, PG_ANALYST_SYNTHESIS_DISCLOSED_KEEP=1 in the sweep env (PG_ANALYST_SYNTHESIS_BASKET_FULLTEXT and PG_SYNTH_RENDER_CLEAN already default ON, so inline [confidence:] markers are stripped and summarized). ONE small gated code change riding along: PG_SYNTH_DISCLOSURE_SCHOLARLY (default OFF) rewrites ANALYST_SYNTHESIS_DISCLOSURE (analyst_synthesis.py:64-69) from audit-machinery language ('not individually span-verified... not audit-grade claims') to honest scholarly framing ('This section is the authors' interpretive synthesis of the cited evidence above') — same disclosure semantics, without shipping a self-indictment into the dimension graded for synthesis.
CLAIMS: +1.0 to +2.0 overall. Insight 0.42 -> ~0.47-0.48 is worth +1.6; discounted for the prior negative on insight *directives* — but this is new admitted CONTENT, not a directive to the same forbidden writer. on insight (0.32) — our worst dimension (0.4238) under the highest weight (w=1.5)
FAILURE MODE THEY ADMIT: (a) Word budget: the section targets 1500-3000 words and we are already AT reference length — without C6's padding purge this trades density for length, the exact Rank7 failure. (b) The writer (reasoning-first) has historically returned empty content in drb_72 (#1323 dark-synthesis trap) — the lane can silently emit nothing. (c) Hedged interpretive prose the judge reads as waffle, repeating the insight-directive negative.
NEW FAITHFULNESS CONTRACT: TWO-TIER ADMISSION CONTRACT. TIER F (FACT) — unchanged, byte-identical when all new flags are off: a sentence is admitted iff it clears every existing leg of verify_sentence_provenance (provenance_generator.py:2098-2959) and `is_verified = len(failures) == 0` (:2930). TIER I (INFERENCE, body, PG_STRICT_VERIFY_INFERENCE_TIER): a sentence reaching the NEUTRAL branch (:2596-2621) is admitted iff ALL of: (1) it cites >=2 distinct evidence rows via provenance tokens — untokened sentences still die at :2186-2193, unchanged; (2) every MECHANICAL leg already passed — these run before entailment and are untouched, so every number, percent, integer, and binding qualifier in the sentence is already verified against the cited spans; (3) the first judge verdict was NEUTRAL — CONTRADICTED remains unconditionally fatal (:2607-2615 posture preserved); (4) the sentence carries an interpretive hedge cue (_INTERPRETIVE_CUE_RE, analyst_synthesis_deviation_check.py:219-226) — a bare categorical assertion is ineligible; (5) no named entity (author, study, organization, venue) appears in the sentence that is absent from the union of the cited rows' full text (reusing _span_grounds_sentence:138-152 and the author-attribution floor at :264-302); (6) a SECOND judge call with a premise-decomposition prompt returns INFERENCE_OK, defined as: every factual assertion in the SENTENCE is present in the SPAN, and the only content not in the SPAN is the hedged interpretive relation over those assertions — any other verdict, and any transport error, FAILS CLOSED to the existing entailment_failed drop (:2919-2923 posture preserved). Every Tier-I keep is tagged soft_warning='inference_tier' in the audit log and counted in telemetry, so the audit can enumerate exactly which sentences carry interpretation. TIER I-S (analyst section, existing dark-lane contract, unchanged from its authors' design): cited synthesis passes the deterministic anti-fabrication floor (every number in the union of cited sources' full text, >=2 content-word overlap); uncited interpretation passes _is_disclosable_interpretation (no decimals, no percent, no quantitative claim, no attribution absent from the pool, hedge cue mandatory); fabricated attributions and unhedged categoricals DROP; the section ships under an explicit interpretive disclosure. WHAT STILL GETS KILLED, everywhere, in every tier: any sentence contradicted by its spans; any unhedged interpretive assertion; any sentence introducing a number, percent, or named entity not groundable in the union of its cited sources; any untokened body sentence; anything on judge transport error. WHY NO HALLUCINATION HOLE OPENS: the only novel content class admitted anywhere is a HEDGED INTERPRETIVE RELATION whose operands are each independently span-verified — numeric and entity fabrication remain blocked by deterministic legs that run BEFORE and independently OF the judge; the change converts exactly one verdict path (NEUTRAL + hedged + premise-verified) from drop to labeled-keep, and every such keep is individually enumerable in the audit log for post-hoc inspection.

ATTACK THROUGH THIS LENS:
HALLUCINATION HOLE: the two-tier contract exempts inference sentences from span-grounding. Show the concrete path by which an UNGROUNDED or FALSE claim now reaches the report. If faithfulness can regress, this design is unshippable — POLARIS's whole moat is that it does not hallucinate. Be specific.

- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- dataviz: Use this skill whenever you are about to create ANY chart, graph, plot, dashboard, or data visualization, in ANY output medium — an HTML or React artifact, inline SVG, plotting code in any library (matplotlib, plotly, d3, Recharts, …), an image/PNG you will render and upload, or a chart shared into Slack. Read it BEFORE writing the first line of chart code, choosing chart colors, building a stat tile / meter / KPI row, or laying out a dashboard. Produces visualizations that read as one system — elegant, accessible, consistent in light and dark — using a brand-neutral placeholder palette you swap for your own. Teaches a design-system-agnostic method: a form heuristic, a color formula with a runnable validator, mark specs, and interaction rules. A validated default palette is documented in `references/palette.md` — swap that file's values for your brand's. Triggers on: "chart", "graph", "plot", "data viz", "visualization", "dashboard", "analytics", "visualize data", "categorical colors", "sequential / diverging palette", "stat tile", "sparkline", "heatmap", "legend", "axis", "tooltip", "chart colors", "color by series".
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by exercising it end-to-end and observing behavior — drive the affected flow, not just tests or typecheck. Run before committing nontrivial changes; bootstraps this repo's project verify skill if none exists yet. Don't invoke it on a diff that only touches tests, docs, or other code with no runtime surface to drive (a change to product source always has one) — there's nothing to observe.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings; ultra: deep multi-agent review in the cloud). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled cloud agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring cloud agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Fable, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a GitHub pull request; for your working diff use /code-review
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

3	THE PROBLEM (forensic): ``analyst_synthesis.generate_analyst_synthesis`` is architecturally OUTSIDE
4	the faithfulness engine — its system prompt FORBIDS ``[#ev:]`` span tokens and cites by ``[N]``
5	bibliography markers only, so the per-sentence ``strict_verify`` entailment gate NEVER sees it. It is
6	the longest, most confident block in the report, and its grounded over-assertions (hedges upgraded to
7	assertions, interpolated specifics, model-asserted author names absent from the pool) ship UNGATED.
8	
9	THE FIX (the operator-locked "verify AFTER compose = LABEL, never DELETE" pattern, §-1.3 BASKET
10	FAITHFULNESS / `feedback_always_release_verifier_labels_never_holds_2026_06_14`): this module is a
11	thin, additive DEVIATION CHECK over the already-composed synthesis prose. It:
12	
13	  1. splits the synthesis into sentences (reusing ``provenance_generator.split_into_sentences``),
14	  2. for each sentence, parses its ``[N]`` markers (``report_claim_extractor._NUMBERED_RE``) and
15	     resolves ``N -> bibliography[N-1].evidence_id -> the cited evidence row's span``,
16	  3. asks a groundedness judge (the certified MiniMax-M2 Sentinel decomposition by default — a SECOND
17	     family vs the GLM/DeepSeek writer, so two-family holds) whether the cited span SUPPORTS the
18	     sentence,
19	  4. for an UNSUPPORTED sentence appends an inline confidence marker
20	     (``claim_labeler.render_confidence_marker(BUCKET_LOW)``), and for a sentence whose ``[N]`` resolve
21	     NO span (``BUCKET_NO_SOURCE``) — KEEP the sentence, NEVER delete it.
22	
23	FAIL-CLOSED to ``BUCKET_LOW``: a judge error / transport flap / timeout LABELS the sentence low (over-
24	labeling is the SAFE direction for a previously-ungated layer), never a silent high, never a hold.
25	
26	FAITHFULNESS ENGINE UNTOUCHED: ``strict_verify`` / NLI / 4-role D8 / provenance / span-grounding are
27	NOT modified. This is an additive, LABEL-only deviation leg over the previously-ungated synthesis
28	prose. The judge here is ADVISORY — it never drops a sentence, never changes ``is_verified``.
29	
30	LAW VI: the leg is gated default-ON by ``PG_ANALYST_SYNTHESIS_DEVIATION_CHECK`` and shares the coarse
31	``PG_SWEEP_ANALYST_SYNTHESIS`` kill-switch (when the whole analyst layer is off, this never runs).
32	
33	TESTABILITY / NO HANG (I-arch-006/007 lesson): the per-sentence groundedness call is INJECTABLE
34	(``judge_fn``) so a test passes a deterministic fake; the real path resolves a bounded, deadline-wrapped

95	_MIN_CONTENT_OVERLAP = 2
96	_MARKER_RE = re.compile(r"\[[^\]]*\]")          # [N] citation + [confidence:...] markers
97	# I-deepfix-001 D3 P1 (#1344): a pre-labeled sentence (already carrying a [confidence:...] marker —

109	    "not", "no", "but", "if", "then", "than", "so", "such", "into", "over", "under", "about",

129	    return {d.replace(",", "") for d in _DECIMAL_RE.findall(text or "")}

133	    """Lower-cased content tokens (>=3 chars, not a stopword)."""
134	    return {w for w in _WORD_RE.findall(str(text or "").lower())

138	def _span_grounds_sentence(sentence: str, span: str) -> bool:
139	    """Deterministic span-grounding leg of the D3 frozen-engine gate (strict_verify §9.1.3 principle).
140	
141	    True iff the sentence's prose is anchored in the cited span: (a) EVERY decimal in the sentence
142	    appears in the span (a synthesis sentence must not invent or alter a number the cited source did
143	    not state), AND (b) the sentence and the span share >= ``_MIN_CONTENT_OVERLAP`` content words. A
144	    blank span never grounds. PURE. This is a CONSERVATIVE gate — it can only REFUSE to promote (drop
145	    to the fail-closed audit tail); it never admits a sentence the judge rejected."""
146	    if not span or not span.strip():
147	        return False
148	    core = _strip_markers(sentence)
149	    sent_nums = _decimals(core)
150	    if sent_nums and not sent_nums.issubset(_decimals(span)):
151	        return False
152	    return len(_content_tokens(core) & _content_tokens(span)) >= _MIN_CONTENT_OVERLAP

206	# An author attribution ("Acemoglu and Restrepo", "Brynjolfsson et al") inside an UNCITED interpretive
207	# sentence is disclosable ONLY if EVERY named surname is actually in the evidence pool (a real study being
208	# interpreted). A surname absent from the pool is a fabricated attribution and must DROP.
209	_AUTHOR_ATTR_RE = re.compile(r"\b[A-Z][a-z]{2,}\s+(?:et al|and\s+[A-Z][a-z]{2,})")
210	_SURNAME_RE = re.compile(r"[A-Z][a-z]{2,}")
211	_POOL_TOKEN_RE = re.compile(r"[a-z]{3,}")
212	# Codex + Fable depth-gate P1 (load-bearing §-1.3 fix): a disclosable NON-header sentence must carry a
213	# HEDGE / INTERPRETIVE cue — the markers the composer's own system prompt tells the LLM to use for hedged
214	# interpretation (suggests / implies / may / typically / broadly / consistent with / framework / logic /
215	# depends on / …). A bare UNHEDGED categorical assertion ("Generative AI will replace all lawyers") carries
216	# no hedge => NOT disclosable => drops. Deliberately EXCLUDES bare analytical nouns (trend / pattern /
217	# evidence) that a fabricated declarative could carry. Verified to still admit box2's real analytical
218	# framework tissue.
219	_INTERPRETIVE_CUE_RE = re.compile(
220	    r"\b(?:suggest\w*|impl(?:y|ies|ied|ication\w*)|may|might|could|consistent with|typical\w*|broad\w*|"
221	    r"appears?|likely|interpret\w*|framework\w*|mechanism\w*|logic\w*|depends?\s+on|reflect\w*|align\w*|"
222	    r"underscore\w*|in principle|plausibl\w*|tends?\s+to|contextualiz\w*|represent\w*|modulat\w*|"
223	    r"reinforc\w*|poses?\b|requir\w*|nuance\w*|distinction\w*|dimension\w*|paradox\w*|conceptual\w*|"
224	    r"scaffold\w*|thesis|hypothes\w*|unresolved|underexplored|remain\w*|applies\b|extension\b)",
225	    re.IGNORECASE,
226	)
227	# Codex depth-gate iter-2 P1: a contentful CATEGORICAL header ("### Generative AI will replace all lawyers")
228	# must NOT pass as a structural label. A structural header is a NOUN-PHRASE section label with NO
229	# categorical CLAIM verb; a header carrying one is a mislabeled assertion => drops. (box2's real headers —
230	# "Mechanism Interpretation: …", "Positive Views: …", "Challenges: …", "Open Questions …" — carry none.)
231	_CLAIM_VERB_RE = re.compile(
232	    r"\b(?:will|shall|would|eliminat\w*|replac\w*|destroy\w*|caus\w*|guarantee\w*|prove[sd]?|proven|"
233	    r"cure[sd]?|kill\w*|ends?\b|end\s+of)\b",
234	    re.IGNORECASE,
235	)
236	# Codex/Fable depth-gate: a CRISP quantitative claim in ANY form — digit, spelled percent, spelled cardinal
237	# over a domain noun, a fraction "of all X", a "one in N" ratio, or a "millions of X" magnitude — is a
238	# numeric claim that must ground => NOT disclosable. Deliberately does NOT match SOFT quantifiers
239	# ("many/most/some workers") which are legitimate qualitative interpretive prose. Domain noun set is the
240	# labor-market subjects a proportion claim would quantify.
241	_QUANT_DOMAIN = r"(?:jobs|workers|occupations|tasks|employment|roles|people|firms|companies|households|professionals|positions)"
242	_QUANT_CLAIM_RE = re.compile(
243	    r"\bone\s+in\s+(?:a\s+)?(?:two|three|four|five|six|seven|eight|nine|ten|\d+)\b"          # one in five
244	    r"|\b(?:a|one|two|three|four)\s+(?:half|third|quarter|fifth|sixth|seventh|eighth|ninth|tenth)\b"  # a fifth / two thirds
245	    r"|\b(?:half|third|quarter|fifth|sixth|seventh|eighth|ninth|tenth)\s+of\s+(?:all\s+)?" + _QUANT_DOMAIN + r"\b"  # BARE "half of all jobs" / "quarter of workers"
246	    r"|\b(?:millions?|billions?|thousands?|hundreds?)\s+of\s+" + _QUANT_DOMAIN + r"\b"        # millions of workers
247	    r"|\b(?:one|two|three|four|five|six|seven|eight|nine|ten|eleven|twelve|thirteen|fourteen|fifteen|"
248	    r"sixteen|seventeen|eighteen|nineteen|twenty|thirty|forty|fifty|sixty|seventy|eighty|ninety|"
249	    r"hundred|thousand|million|billion)\b[\w\s-]{0,20}\b(?:of\s+(?:all\s+)?)?" + _QUANT_DOMAIN + r"\b",  # spelled N … jobs
250	    re.IGNORECASE,
251	)

254	def _build_pool_surnames(evidence_rows: "list[dict[str, Any]]") -> "set[str]":
255	    """Word-boundary TOKEN set of the pool's author/title words (len>=3), for the author-attribution guard.
256	    Token membership (not substring) so a fabricated 'Autor et al' can't pass via 'autor' ⊂ 'author'."""

264	def _is_disclosable_interpretation(sentence: str, pool_surnames: "set[str]") -> bool:
265	    """True iff an UNCITED synthesis sentence is safe to render under the disclosed analyst label — a HEDGED
266	    QUALITATIVE interpretation carrying NO ungrounded SPECIFIC claim. Faithfulness guards (applied to headers
267	    TOO): any decimal / percent / integer / year => a numeric claim that MUST ground => NOT disclosable; an
268	    author attribution with ANY surname absent from the pool token-set => a fabricated study => NOT
269	    disclosable. A structural section header (no number, no bad author) is disclosable. A NON-header sentence
270	    ALSO requires a HEDGE/INTERPRETIVE cue — a bare unhedged categorical assertion is NOT disclosable.
271	    §-1.3: hedged qualitative interpretation is disclosed under an honest 'not span-verified' label; numeric,
272	    named, and bare-categorical claims still drop."""
273	    raw = _strip_markers(sentence).strip()
274	    if not raw:
275	        return False
276	    is_header = raw.startswith("#")
277	    core = raw.lstrip("#").strip()
278	    if not core:
279	        return False
280	    # numeric guard — applies to HEADERS too (Codex P1-2): a number/percent/year must ground, never disclose.
281	    if _decimals(core):
282	        return False
283	    # ANY percent/percentage word is a quantitative claim that must ground — a robust catch-all covering
284	    # every digit AND spelled form ("40%" / "forty percent" / "fifteen percent"), ending the spelled-number
285	    # enumeration (Codex depth-gate iter-3/4). Real qualitative interpretation never states a bare percent.
286	    if "percent" in core.lower():
287	        return False
288	    # any CRISP quantitative claim without the word "percent" ("a fifth of all jobs", "one in five workers",
289	    # "millions of workers", "fifteen … jobs") — same numeric-claim class; soft quantifiers (many/most) pass.
290	    if _QUANT_CLAIM_RE.search(core):
291	        return False
292	    # author-attribution guard — EVERY named surname (first + co-authors) must be a pool TOKEN (Codex/Fable P2).
293	    for m in _AUTHOR_ATTR_RE.finditer(core):
294	        if any(name.lower() not in pool_surnames for name in _SURNAME_RE.findall(m.group(0))):
295	            return False
296	    if is_header:
297	        # Codex iter-2 P1: a structural section-label header is disclosable, but a contentful CATEGORICAL
298	        # header ("### Generative AI will replace all lawyers") is a mislabeled assertion => drops.
299	        return not bool(_CLAIM_VERB_RE.search(core))
300	    # Codex/Fable P1-1: a NON-header interpretive sentence must actually be HEDGED interpretation, not a bare
301	    # unhedged categorical assertion.
302	    return bool(_INTERPRETIVE_CUE_RE.search(core))
303

334	def _resolve_span_for_evidence_id(
335	    evidence_id: str, evidence_rows: list[dict[str, Any]], *, full_text: bool = False
336	) -> str:
337	    """Resolve an evidence_id to its cited span text (``direct_quote`` first, then ``statement``).
338	    Returns ``""`` when the id is not in the pool or carries no span text. wave-2 (#1370): ``full_text``
339	    widens the resolved text to the source's TRUSTED evidence-BODY fields (``direct_quote`` + ``statement``)
340	    so a number/word that lives in the source body but not the exact quote slice grounds — the box2
341	    over-drop where a real Brynjolfsson/Eloundou number sat in the source ``statement`` but not the exact
342	    quote slice, so a grounded synthesis sentence was dropped. Codex depth-gate P1: ``title``/``snippet``
343	    (retrieval METADATA) are DELIBERATELY EXCLUDED so a number that appears only incidentally in a search
344	    snippet or title can NEVER ground a synthesis sentence — the anti-fabrication floor stays honest."""
345	    for row in evidence_rows or []:
346	        rid = str(row.get("evidence_id") or row.get("id") or "")
347	        if rid and rid == evidence_id:
348	            if full_text:
349	                return " ".join(
350	                    str(row.get(k, "") or "") for k in ("direct_quote", "statement")
351	                ).strip()
352	            return str(row.get("direct_quote") or row.get("statement") or "")
353	    return ""

356	def _resolve_sentence_span(
357	    sentence: str,
358	    bibliography: list[dict[str, Any]],
359	    evidence_rows: list[dict[str, Any]],
360	    *,
361	    full_text: bool = False,
362	) -> str:
363	    """Concatenate the cited spans for ALL ``[N]`` markers in ``sentence``. ``N`` indexes the
364	    bibliography 1-based -> ``bibliography[N-1].evidence_id`` -> the evidence row's span. Returns
365	    ``""`` when the sentence carries NO resolvable ``[N]`` span (=> the caller labels BUCKET_NO_SOURCE).
366	    wave-2 (#1370): ``full_text`` resolves each cited source to its WHOLE text (see
367	    ``_resolve_span_for_evidence_id``) so a synthesis sentence grounds against the whole-basket union,
368	    not the narrow direct_quote slice. PURE (no I/O)."""
369	    spans: list[str] = []
370	    for m in _NUMBERED_RE.finditer(sentence):
371	        try:
372	            n = int(m.group(1))
373	        except (TypeError, ValueError):
374	            continue
375	        if n < 1 or n > len(bibliography or []):
376	            continue
377	        entry = bibliography[n - 1]
378	        eid = str(entry.get("evidence_id") or "")
379	        if not eid:
380	            continue
381	        span = _resolve_span_for_evidence_id(eid, evidence_rows, full_text=full_text)
382	        if span:
383	            spans.append(span)
384	    return "\n".join(spans)

387	def _resolve_sentence_ev_rows(
388	    sentence: str,
389	    bibliography: list[dict[str, Any]],
390	    evidence_rows: list[dict[str, Any]],
391	) -> list[dict[str, Any]]:
392	    """Resolve the EVIDENCE ROW dict for every ``[N]`` marker in ``sentence``. ``N`` indexes the
393	    bibliography 1-based -> ``bibliography[N-1].evidence_id`` -> the matching evidence row. Returns the
394	    list of cited rows (deduped by evidence_id, order-preserving) so the D3 frozen-engine re-pass can
395	    build a real ``[#ev:id:start-end]`` provenance token per cited source. Rows with no span text are
396	    skipped. PURE (no I/O)."""
397	    seen: set[str] = set()
398	    rows: list[dict[str, Any]] = []
399	    for m in _NUMBERED_RE.finditer(sentence):
400	        try:
401	            n = int(m.group(1))
402	        except (TypeError, ValueError):
403	            continue
404	        if n < 1 or n > len(bibliography or []):
405	            continue
406	        eid = str((bibliography[n - 1] or {}).get("evidence_id") or "")
407	        if not eid or eid in seen:
408	            continue
409	        for row in evidence_rows or []:
410	            rid = str(row.get("evidence_id") or row.get("id") or "")
411	            if rid and rid == eid:
412	                if str(row.get("direct_quote") or row.get("statement") or "").strip():
413	                    seen.add(eid)
414	                    rows.append(row)
415	                break
416	    return rows

419	def _frozen_engine_verifies_sentence(
420	    base_sentence: str, cited_rows: list[dict[str, Any]]
421	) -> bool:
422	    """D3 P0 (#1344) — re-pass a promoted synthesis sentence through the FROZEN faithfulness engine.
423	
424	    Codex iter-4 P0: the analyst-synthesis writer cites by ``[N]`` bibliography markers, so the
425	    per-sentence ``strict_verify`` / NLI entailment gate never saw it — promoted synthesis prose entered
426	    the scored body without the same provenance gate every body claim clears. This function CLOSES that
427	    hole: it deterministically rebuilds a real ``[#ev:<key>:<start>-<end>]`` provenance token for EACH
428	    cited source (the full cited span) and calls the UNCHANGED ``verify_sentence_provenance`` — the exact
429	    strict_verify (numeric-in-span + >=2 content-word overlap + percent-role + trial-name +
430	    overstatement) AND NLI-entailment engine every body claim passes. The sentence is admissible to the
431	    body ONLY if the frozen engine returns ``is_verified``.
432	
433	    Synthetic token-safe keys (``s0``, ``s1`` ...) are used because a real evidence_id may contain
434	    characters ``_PROVENANCE_TOKEN_RE`` ([A-Za-z0-9_]+) rejects; the key identity is irrelevant to the
435	    engine (it grounds against the SPAN TEXT, keyed by the token's id in the pool). The full evidence row
436	    is carried into the pool (so the engine's shell / trial-name / attribution legs read real fields);
437	    only ``direct_quote`` is pinned to the resolved span text so the span offsets are exact.
438	
439	    The engine is NOT modified — this is an ADDITIVE re-pass on top of the frozen gate. FAIL-CLOSED: any
440	    resolution / engine fault returns ``False`` (the sentence drops from the body), never a silent True."""
441	    if not cited_rows:
442	        return False
443	    try:
444	        evidence_pool: dict[str, dict[str, Any]] = {}
445	        token_parts: list[str] = []
446	        for idx, row in enumerate(cited_rows):
447	            span = str(row.get("direct_quote") or row.get("statement") or "")
448	            if not span.strip():
449	                continue
450	            key = f"s{idx}"
451	            pool_row = dict(row)
452	            pool_row["direct_quote"] = span
453	            evidence_pool[key] = pool_row
454	            token_parts.append(f"[#ev:{key}:0-{len(span)}]")
455	        if not evidence_pool:
456	            return False
457	        # Strip the writer's [N] / [confidence:] brackets from the prose BEFORE appending the real
458	        # provenance tokens, so the engine reads the claim prose (never a bracket digit as a claim
459	        # number) plus exactly the reconstructed [#ev] tokens.
460	        prose = _strip_markers(base_sentence).strip()
461	        if not prose:
462	            return False
463	        converted = f"{prose} {' '.join(token_parts)}"
464	        result = verify_sentence_provenance(converted, evidence_pool)
465	        return bool(getattr(result, "is_verified", False))
466	    except Exception as exc:  # fail-closed: an engine/wiring fault DROPS the sentence, never admits it
467	        logger.warning(
468	            "[analyst_deviation] D3 frozen-engine re-pass faulted (fail-closed DROP): %s", exc,
469	        )
470	        return False

497	def _default_sentinel_judge() -> "Callable[[str, str], bool]":
498	    """Build the real groundedness judge: the certified MiniMax-M2 Sentinel decomposition (a SECOND
499	    family vs the GLM/DeepSeek writer, so two-family holds). Returns a ``judge_fn(claim, span) -> bool``
500	    where True == the span SUPPORTS the claim. Resolved LAZILY so importing this module never
501	    constructs a transport. FAIL-CLOSED: any construction / call failure surfaces as ``False`` (=>
502	    BUCKET_LOW), never a silent True."""
503	    def _judge(claim: str, span: str) -> bool:
504	        if not span.strip():
505	            return False
506	        try:
507	            from src.polaris_graph.roles.sentinel_adapter import (
508	                _configured_sentinel_slug,
509	                run_sentinel,
510	            )
511	            from src.polaris_graph.roles.role_transport import EvidenceDocument
512	            from src.polaris_graph.roles.openrouter_role_transport import (
513	                OpenRouterRoleTransport,
514	                _default_role_http_client,
515	            )
516	            from src.polaris_graph.roles.sentinel_contract import SentinelVerdict
517	        except Exception as exc:  # pragma: no cover — wiring resolved at runtime on the paid path
518	            logger.warning("[analyst_deviation] sentinel imports unavailable (fail-closed LOW): %s", exc)
519	            return False
520	        try:
521	            slug = _configured_sentinel_slug()
522	            if not slug:
523	                logger.warning("[analyst_deviation] no Sentinel slug configured (fail-closed LOW)")
524	                return False
525	            transport = OpenRouterRoleTransport(_default_role_http_client())
526	            result, _records = run_sentinel(
527	                transport,
528	                claim,
529	                [EvidenceDocument(doc_id="span", text=span)],
530	                model_slug=slug,
531	            )
532	            # GROUNDED + a clean parse == supported. UNGROUNDED OR a fail-closed parse == NOT supported
533	            # (BUCKET_LOW). NEVER trust a non-clean parse as supported (lethal-inversion guard).
534	            return bool(result.verdict == SentinelVerdict.GROUNDED and result.parsed_ok)
535	        except Exception as exc:  # transport flap / cap-adjacent / parse error -> fail-closed LOW
536	            logger.warning("[analyst_deviation] sentinel call failed (fail-closed LOW): %s", exc)
537	            return False
538	    return _judge

541	def screen_synthesis_against_baskets(
542	    text: str,
543	    bibliography: list[dict[str, Any]],
544	    evidence_rows: list[dict[str, Any]],
545	    *,
546	    judge_fn: "Callable[[str, str], bool] | None" = None,
547	) -> "tuple[str, dict[str, int]]":
548	    """Bring the analyst-synthesis layer UNDER the faithfulness engine. Returns ``(body_text, telemetry)``.
549	
550	    Two modes, one gate flag (``PG_ANALYST_SYNTHESIS_PROMOTE_GROUNDED``):
551	
552	    * D3 FAIL-CLOSED (promote flag ON) — the gate that lets the analyst layer turn ON safely. A
553	      synthesis sentence renders in ``body_text`` ONLY if it passes BOTH legs of the frozen engine —
554	      the deterministic span-grounding leg (``_span_grounds_sentence``: numbers + >=2 content-word
555	      overlap vs the cited span) AND the D8/NLI groundedness judge. A passing sentence is
556	      KEEP-and-PROMOTE'd (BUCKET_MODERATE). A sentence that FAILS either leg — ungrounded, no-source,
557	      or judge-fault — is DROPPED from ``body_text`` (fail-closed) and surfaced only in the drop
558	      telemetry + a fail-LOUD log, NEVER rendered as a hedged body claim.
559	
560	    * LEGACY ADVISORY (promote flag OFF, default) — byte-identical KEEP-and-LABEL: no-source sentences
561	      get a BUCKET_NO_SOURCE marker, judge-unsupported sentences a BUCKET_LOW marker, supported
562	      sentences pass bare. Nothing is dropped.
563	
564	    For each synthesis sentence the ``[N]`` markers resolve to cited evidence spans. ``judge_fn``
565	    (``(claim, span) -> bool``, True == supported) is INJECTABLE for offline tests; the default lazily
566	    builds the certified-Sentinel judge. FAIL-CLOSED on any judge fault (LABEL LOW in legacy mode; DROP
567	    in D3 mode — both the safe direction). NEVER touches ``strict_verify`` / NLI / 4-role (this gate is
568	    ADDED on top of the frozen engine). Returns the input unchanged (telemetry zeroed) when disabled."""
569	    telemetry = {
570	        "synthesis_deviation_labeled_count": 0,
571	        "synthesis_deviation_unresolved_count": 0,
572	        "synthesis_deviation_promoted_count": 0,
573	        "synthesis_deviation_dropped_count": 0,
574	    }
575	    if not text or not text.strip() or not deviation_check_enabled():
576	        return text, telemetry
577	
578	    # I-deepfix-001 M6 (Layer 2): default-OFF PROMOTE mode — a grounded sentence is positively labeled
579	    # (KEEP-and-PROMOTE) instead of passing bare. Resolved once; OFF => grounded sentences stay bare.
580	    promote = promote_grounded_enabled()
581	
582	    if judge_fn is None:
583	        judge_fn = _default_sentinel_judge()
584	
585	    sentences = split_into_sentences(text)
586	    if not sentences:
587	        return text, telemetry
588	
589	    # Resolve each sentence's cited span ONCE (pure). A sentence with no resolvable span never calls
590	    # the judge (it is a BUCKET_NO_SOURCE label, not an UNSUPPORTED verdict). Span resolution reads the
591	    # ``[N]`` markers, which are untouched by the confidence-marker strip below.
592	    spans = [_resolve_sentence_span(s, bibliography, evidence_rows) for s in sentences]
593	    # I-deepfix-001 wave-2 (#1370) basket faithfulness (§-1.3): in basket mode, ALSO resolve each sentence's
594	    # cited sources to their WHOLE-basket full text (direct_quote + statement + title + snippet), unioned
595	    # across all cited [N], so a synthesis sentence whose grounded numbers/words live anywhere in its cited
596	    # sources is not falsely dropped for citing only the narrow direct_quote slice (box2 over-drop; harness
597	    # T2). A number in NO cited source still drops (harness T3). The legacy 4-leg path keeps the narrow
598	    # `spans`. Default-ON within basket mode; PG_ANALYST_SYNTHESIS_BASKET_FULLTEXT=0 reverts to the narrow union.
599	    basket_spans = (
600	        [_resolve_sentence_span(s, bibliography, evidence_rows, full_text=True) for s in sentences]
601	        if (basket_mode_enabled() and _basket_fulltext_enabled())
602	        else spans

613	    ]

615	    # I-deepfix-001 D3 P1 (#1344): screen the BARE prose. A pre-labeled sentence (already carrying a
616	    # ``[confidence:...]`` marker) has that marker stripped so the judge + span-grounding legs see the
617	    # claim itself, never a self-asserted confidence tag. For an un-labeled sentence this is a no-op
618	    # (byte-identical). In PROMOTE mode the stripped form is what re-enters the gate and is re-labeled;
619	    # the legacy path still short-circuits on the ORIGINAL sentence (idempotent keep-unchanged).
620	    screen_sentences = [_strip_confidence_markers(s) for s in sentences]
621	
622	    # BOUNDED-PARALLEL groundedness for the sentences that DO resolve a span (never a serial unbounded
623	    # loop). A per-future deadline fail-CLOSES to "not supported" (=> BUCKET_LOW).
624	    supported: dict[int, bool] = {}
625	    judge_indices = [i for i, sp in enumerate(spans) if sp.strip()]
626	    if judge_indices:

629	        # pool, so the batch is bounded by ONE TOTAL wall deadline, never the sum of
630	        # per-future deadlines. AND the pool is shut down with wait=False +
631	        # cancel_futures=True so a Sentinel worker still stuck in a slow role-
632	        # transport call is ABANDONED — the `with`-block shutdown(wait=True) would
633	        # otherwise re-block on the timed-out worker and stall the synthesis return.
634	        # Every unresolved sentence fail-CLOSES to LOW (not supported).
635	        pool = ThreadPoolExecutor(max_workers=_max_inflight())
636	        try:
637	            futures = {
638	                pool.submit(judge_fn, screen_sentences[i], spans[i]): i for i in judge_indices
639	            }
640	            _end = time.monotonic() + deadline
641	            for fut, i in futures.items():
642	                _remaining = max(0.0, _end - time.monotonic())
643	                try:
644	                    supported[i] = bool(fut.result(timeout=_remaining))
645	                except (_FutureTimeout, Exception) as exc:  # fail-closed LOW on any judge fault
646	                    logger.warning(
647	                        "[analyst_deviation] groundedness judge fault on sentence %d "
648	                        "(fail-closed LOW): %s", i, exc,
649	                    )
650	                    supported[i] = False

652	            # Never block the synthesis return on a stuck judge worker.
653	            pool.shutdown(wait=False, cancel_futures=True)

655	        # fail-closed to LOW (covers the case where the loop exhausted the wall
656	        # budget before reaching a later future).
657	        for i in judge_indices:
658	            supported.setdefault(i, False)

662	    for i, sentence in enumerate(sentences):
663	        has_span = bool(spans[i].strip())

665	        if promote:
666	            # ── D3 FAIL-CLOSED gate ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

672	            #       and the UNCHANGED strict_verify (numeric-in-span + >=2 content-word overlap +

674	            #       is_verified. THIS is the leg that was missing — a [N]-cited synthesis sentence now

677	            #       superset guard, kept so a sentence can never be promoted on the judge alone), AND
678	            #   (3) the D8/NLI groundedness judge (supported[i], the 4-role Sentinel leg).
679	            # Anything else — no cited span, engine says unverified, judge says unsupported, or a
680	            # judge/engine fault (fail-closed to False) — is DROPPED from the body and collected for the
681	            # fail-loud audit.
682	            #
683	            # P1 (#1344): a PRE-LABELED sentence does NOT get an idempotent free pass here — it is
684	            # re-screened on its bare prose (``base`` = confidence-marker stripped). An ungrounded
685	            # sentence that arrived already carrying a ``[confidence: low]`` tag is therefore DROPPED,
686	            # not admitted as a hedged body claim. A grounded one is re-promoted with a fresh moderate
687	            # marker on the stripped base (never a double marker).
688	            base = screen_sentences[i]
689	            if basket_mode_enabled():
690	                # P1 basket mode: KEEP grounded cross-source synthesis. The deterministic anti-fabrication
691	                # floor (numbers-in-union + >=2 content-word overlap vs the UNION of cited spans) is the
692	                # faithfulness gate; the extractive single-span ENTAILMENT requirement is dropped (it
693	                # over-kills interpretive synthesis). Fabricated numbers + no-citation sentences still drop.
694	                # wave-2 (#1370): ground against the WHOLE-basket FULL-TEXT union of the cited sources
695	                # (basket_spans[i]) so a real number living in a source's statement/title — not the narrow
696	                # quote slice — is not falsely dropped (box2 over-drop of the cross-source analytical layer).
697	                _bspan = basket_spans[i]
698	                grounded = bool(_bspan.strip()) and _span_grounds_sentence(base, _bspan)
699	            else:
700	                grounded = (
701	                    has_span
702	                    and supported.get(i, False)
703	                    and _span_grounds_sentence(base, spans[i])
704	                    and _frozen_engine_verifies_sentence(base, cited_rows_per_sentence[i])
705	                )
706	            if grounded:
707	                marker = claim_labeler.render_confidence_marker(claim_labeler.BUCKET_MODERATE)
708	                out_sentences.append(f"{base} {marker}")
709	                telemetry["synthesis_deviation_promoted_count"] += 1
710	            elif _disclose_analyst and _is_disclosable_interpretation(base, _pool_surnames):
711	                # wave-2 (#1370) DEPTH — verify-after-compose = LABEL, never DELETE: a hedged, uncited,
712	                # PURELY-QUALITATIVE interpretive sentence (the analytical connective tissue the composer is
713	                # DESIGNED to write uncited) renders under the analyst-layer DISCLOSURE instead of dropping.
714	                # A fabrication (uncited + a specific number, or a named study absent from the pool) does NOT
715	                # reach here — _is_disclosable_interpretation returns False for it — so it still drops below.
716	                marker = claim_labeler.render_confidence_marker(claim_labeler.BUCKET_NO_SOURCE)
717	                out_sentences.append(f"{base} {marker}")

719	                    telemetry.get("synthesis_deviation_disclosed_count", 0) + 1
720	                )
721	            else:
722	                dropped.append(sentence)
723	                telemetry["synthesis_deviation_dropped_count"] += 1
724	            continue

726	        # ── LEGACY ADVISORY KEEP-and-LABEL (promote OFF, default) — byte-identical ────────────────

728	        # gate above deliberately re-screens pre-labeled sentences rather than pass them through).
729	        if _ALREADY_LABELED_MARKER in sentence:

732	        if not has_span:
733	            # No resolvable cited [N] span at all -> a genuinely uncited / unresolvable claim.
734	            marker = claim_labeler.render_confidence_marker(claim_labeler.BUCKET_NO_SOURCE)
735	            out_sentences.append(f"{sentence} {marker}")
736	            telemetry["synthesis_deviation_unresolved_count"] += 1
737	            continue
738	        if supported.get(i, False):
739	            out_sentences.append(sentence)  # supported -> KEEP unchanged
740	            continue

742	        marker = claim_labeler.render_confidence_marker(claim_labeler.BUCKET_LOW)
743	        out_sentences.append(f"{sentence} {marker}")
744	        telemetry["synthesis_deviation_labeled_count"] += 1
745	
746	    if dropped:
747	        # Fail-LOUD: the dropped sentences never reach the scored body — surface them so a D3 drop is
748	        # visible in the run log (the typed non-answer audit trail), never a silent deletion.
749	        logger.warning(
750	            "[analyst_deviation] D3 fail-closed: DROPPED %d ungrounded/no-source synthesis sentence(s) "
751	            "from the scored body (retained only in this audit log, never rendered as a body claim): %s",
752	            len(dropped), dropped,
753	        )
754	    return " ".join(out_sentences), telemetry

14	writes 1500-3000 words of interpretive expert commentary. The synthesis:
15	  - is CLEARLY labeled in report.md (not span-verified)

604	def _render_clean_synthesis(text: str) -> str:
605	    """Return the render-cleaned analyst-synthesis text (C5). See module block above.
606	
607	    Pure, no-network, faithfulness-neutral. Returns the input UNCHANGED when the flag is OFF or when
608	    there is nothing to clean (no in-text disclosure copy AND no inline confidence markers)."""
609	    if not text or not text.strip() or not _render_clean_enabled():
610	        return text
611	    had_disclosure = ANALYST_SYNTHESIS_DISCLOSURE in text
612	    buckets = [b.lower() for b in _RENDER_CLEAN_CONF_BUCKET_RE.findall(text)]
613	    if not had_disclosure and not buckets:
614	        return text  # already clean -> byte-identical
615	    cleaned = text
616	    if had_disclosure:

618	        cleaned = cleaned.replace(ANALYST_SYNTHESIS_DISCLOSURE, "")
619	    if buckets:

621	    # Tidy whitespace the strips left behind (trailing spaces, >2 consecutive blank lines, leading blanks).
622	    cleaned = re.sub(r"[ \t]+\n", "\n", cleaned)
623	    cleaned = re.sub(r"\n{3,}", "\n\n", cleaned).lstrip("\n")
624	    if buckets:
625	        counts = Counter(buckets)
626	        ordered = [f"{counts[b]} {b}" for b in _RENDER_CLEAN_BUCKET_ORDER if counts.get(b)]
627	        for b in counts:  # any non-standard label -> keep it, appended after the known order
628	            if b not in _RENDER_CLEAN_BUCKET_ORDER:
629	                ordered.append(f"{counts[b]} {b}")
630	        note = (
631	            "_Confidence labels for this interpretive section: "
632	            + ", ".join(ordered)
633	            + ". Each was a per-sentence groundedness check, summarized here; the whole section is "
634	            "interpretive and not individually span-verified._"
635	        )
636	        cleaned = f"{note}\n\n{cleaned}" if cleaned.strip() else note
637	    return cleaned
